title: Spreewald Day Trip: Quick Summary
items: **The Spreewald trains do not leave from Hauptbahnhof or Alexanderplatz.** RE2 and RE7 run from Berlin Ostkreuz to Lübbenau in about an hour; RE2 also calls at Gesundbrunnen, RE7 at Ostbahnhof.

**Lübbenau is outside the Berlin ABC zones.** A Deutschlandticket (63 euros a month in 2026) covers the trip; from two people, the Brandenburg-Berlin-Ticket at 36.50 euros a day for up to five is usually the simple answer.

**Punt tours cost 17 to 28 euros in 2026.** Two hours to Lehde 17, three hours with a village stop 20, the 5.5 hour Wotschofska forest tour 28. Boats fill up and leave from about 10:00; there is no timetable.
**A kayak costs about the same as a punt seat.** Around 16 to 22 euros for two hours, 24 to 40 for the day, and the water is flat and calm. Rental stations open around 9:00 in season.
**Lehde is the village with no road access.** You reach it by boat or on a flat 2.6 km path in about 40 minutes. The open-air museum there costs 8 euros; children up to 14 enter free.
**The season runs roughly April to October.** In July and August, go on a weekday and be at the Großer Hafen by 10:30, or the first boats will be full.
